column1: Apply SAP Fiori color palette to my entire Vue app — everything: layouts, components, pages, modals, forms, tables, buttons, inputs, dropdowns, badges, alerts.
Add overflow-hidden to ALL cards, modals, and containers that have rounded corners. This ensures borders clip properly and corners look clean.
<div class=""overflow-hidden rounded-xl bg-[#ffffff] dark:bg-[#232a31] border border-[#d9d9d9] dark:border-[#3a4552]"">
Change: All background, border, text colors, and corner radius with proper overflow-hidden. Every UI element.
Do NOT change: Logic, layout structure, spacing, amber accent #FDB022, green/red status colors, chart colors.
Start with resources/js/Layouts/ — do layout files first
Go file by file — do not batch

458d504a-cf62-4b7c-9d70-b5f56b3de7d4,b13dbd16-2f7a-4c17-979f-e7d7e49ec389,c9339f7a-603f-4c1a-850c-838529f7202f,Add user roles & permissions,Feature,Review,Medium,,"I want to implement a full multi-tenant user roles and permissions system. Before writing any code do the following:

1. ANALYSE FIRST — Read the full database schema, all migrations, all existing models, controllers and routes. Check if there is any existing user/roles/permissions logic already started or partially implemented. Report what you find before doing anything.
2. PLAN — Based on what you find, plan the full implementation:
- Roles table (admin, mechanic, cashier, receptionist etc)
- Permissions table (per page, per action — view only, edit, input, full access)
- How roles integrate with the multi-tenant system (each tenant has their own users and roles, completely isolated)

3. CLIENT LOGIN QUESTION — Right now all clients exist in the system but none can login. We want to add a future feature where some clients can login. Should we add a login_access boolean column to the clients table now so we are prepared? Recommend the best approach and wait for my approval before doing anything about this.
4. APPROVAL — Present the full plan clearly, list every change you will make to the database and codebase, wait for me to approve before writing a single line of code.
- Backend: migrations, models, relationships, middleware, policies, all with maximum security
- Frontend: Users management page accessible only to tenant admin, where you can create users, assign roles, set permissions per page (view only / edit / full access / no access)
- Make sure regular users cannot access the users management page
- All permission checks must happen on both frontend and backend, never trust frontend alone

- Everything must work correctly in the multi-tenant architecture, users and roles must be scoped to their tenant only, no cross-tenant data leaks ever",[],[],false,2026-04-05 17:30:46.816+00,2026-04-09 21:42:24.441+00,[],,false,,,,0,{}

Act as my Security Expert and perform a comprehensive security audit of my entire Vue.js / Laravel application. Identify vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and security best practice violations.
[ ] Brute force protection (rate limiting on login)
[ ] Route guards — are all protected routes actually protected?
[ ] API endpoint authorization — can users access other users' data?
[ ] Password reset flow security (token expiration, email verification)
[ ] SQL injection vulnerabilities (raw queries, unparameterized statements)
[ ] File upload validation (type, size, extension, content verification)
[ ] CORS configuration — is it too permissive?
[ ] Sensitive data exposure in responses (passwords, tokens, PII)
[ ] Error handling — do errors leak stack traces or sensitive info?
[ ] Sensitive data in logs (passwords, credit cards, SSN)
[ ] XSS via v-html (should use v-text or {{ }})
[ ] Insecure direct object references (IDOR) in API calls
[ ] Admin panels exposed without IP restriction
[ ] Outdated Composer packages with known CVEs
[ ] Outdated npm packages with known CVEs
[ ] Typosquatting packages (malicious names similar to popular ones)
[ ] IDOR — can User A access User B's data by changing ID in URL?
[ ] Privilege escalation — can regular user become admin?
[ ] Insecure direct object references in all CRUD operations
[ ] Tenant isolation — can tenants see each other's data?
- Change user ID in URL → should 403
- Change tenant ID in request → should 403
- Access admin route as user → should 403
- Modify hidden form fields → should validate server-side
[ ] Security events logged (failed logins, access denied)
Quick wins (easy fixes with high impact)
Document findings with specific file paths and line numbers
Be thorough. Flag even questionable patterns. Better safe than sorry.",[],[],false,2026-04-05 17:28:03.318+00,2026-04-05 17:28:05.898+00,[],,false,,,,0,{}
